title: The Raise Trap: Why More Pay Makes You Broker
hook: Most people get a raise and end up poorer — here's the math that proves it.
angle: Lifestyle inflation silently devours raises before you even notice, backed by specific dollar amounts for car, rent, and daily spending triggers that expose exactly how it happens.
audience: 25–45 year-olds who got a raise but feel like they never have more money
key_numbers: Average American spends 120% of every raise within 6 months
$400/month car payment eats an entire $4,800 annual raise
